title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and TMAN Designer to manage real-time audio pipelines and enable natural conversational interactions with interruption handling.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers TEN Framework, an open-source system for building real-time voice AI agents that manage streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It centers on interruption handling, the capability that lets an agent be cut off mid-response for more natural conversation. The concrete mechanics: graph-based extensions define the pipeline, and TMAN Designer manages the real-time audio flow.
